lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- At the Airport -- Immigration -- Travel Questions & Answers
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marcos Mansour | Aula 11 -- At the Airport -- Immigration

listenings.text: Good evening, passengers, and welcome to Washington Dulles International Airport. Please have your passport and boarding pass ready for immigration. All arriving passengers must go through passport control before collecting their baggage. If you have nothing to declare, follow the green signs to customs. If you are carrying goods to declare, please use the red channel. The baggage claim for flight two-one-four is at carousel five. Please keep your luggage with you at all times. We wish you a pleasant stay, and thank you for flying with us.

Good evening. May I see your passport, please? Thank you. What is the purpose of your visit? Business or pleasure? I see, a conference. How long are you planning to stay in the United States? And where are you staying during your trip? Do you have a return ticket? Very good. Do you have anything to declare? No? That is fine. Please look at the camera. Thank you. Enjoy your stay in Washington, and welcome to the United States.
